summary:
The Canadian bond market is currently experiencing a 'yield shock' driven by volatility in the US Treasury market following a weak 2-year auction on March 24. While the Bank of Canada maintains a 2.25% policy rate, the Canada 5-year yield has surged toward 3.2%, creating immediate upward pressure on 5-year fixed mortgage rates.

For a Canadian FI, this environment signals a potential compression of lending margins if retail rates are not adjusted to match the rising cost of wholesale funding. The narrowing 2-5 yield spread reflects market uncertainty, requiring careful management of GIC pricing relative to the elevated 2-year benchmark.
